/: FIGHT FOR UNION LABEL

pages.raw_text:
in a curious head to foot survey he
I discovered something that almost
gave him shock. There. in his
hand he held a cOpiOus can of non
union smoking tobacco.


LeClair pushed his way to the
speakers side.


will you kindly lend me this
can for a second?" he Queried.


"Certainly," was the reply.


Gentlemen" LeClair began
When | hear a man arguing the
cause of the union label and of
home industry it thrills me with
a feeling of good fellowship and
brotherly love to all mankind tor
something to that effect but when
| see a man deluded in this style,
(raising tobacco can above head
and pointing to brand beginning
with word 'unionD it conVinces me
that the well meaning public has
not vet learned the tricks of UNI
fair manufacturers. This tobacco
is "scab' stuff. The name of its
brand begins with the word union
in order to delude the public that
desires union made goods


At this juncture a blue coated
cop hove in sight on the horizon
and fearing a pinch fest with him
self on the menu, LeClair cut short
his lecture and disappeared up the
street with the can of tobacco be-
loning to the bewildered friend of
unionism. He says now that the
owner can have same by coming to
the Cooks union hall.
